text.title: ai-design-assistant-patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for ai-design-assistant-patterns as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using modal when the user needs to reference the canvas during AI generation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Modal blocks canvas access; if the user needs canvas context to evaluate the output, use sidebar instead.

failure-mode.description: Using sidebar on mobile-first or narrow-viewport interfaces.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Persistent sidebar on small screens causes context-switch fatigue and reduces canvas working area to an unusable size.

failure-mode.description: AI-generated copy for error states inside the AI assistant UI without human review.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: AI errors inside AI assistants are especially damaging to trust; the copy for these states must be written and reviewed by a human.

failure-mode.description: Agent specifies trigger condition that fires on every selection change.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Over-triggering reduces trust; enforce debounce and explicit user intent in all trigger specs produced by agents.

failure-mode.description: Agent-generated copy for AI assistant UI uses phrases like "Ask me anything!" or "I can help with everything!".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: These phrases set unrealistic expectations and increase user disappointment; human copywriters must review all AI assistant UI copy.

failure-mode.description: Modal pattern spec omits focus trap and keyboard dismiss behavior.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Missing focus trap in modal patterns is an accessibility blocker (WCAG 2.1 SC 2.1.2); agents consistently omit this.
